title: Live Atlas coherence proof v0 (operator human-readable report)
problem: Ticket-285 added opt-in pytest thresholds for live staged orchestrator → private atlas export. We still lack a product-centered operator artifact that answers whether real research output coherently populates the full atlas contract for future frontend use — not just pydantic pass/min counts.
acceptance_criteria: Opt-in live_network operator flow: existing staged orchestrator gates → export private atlas snapshot → emit human-readable coherence report JSON/markdown to temp path.
Report audits population of runs[], nodes[], edges[], cards[], reports[], follow_up_questions[], lineage fields, domain fields, and private-field scan with explicit coherence verdict sections.
Verdict answers: meaningful atlas data from live loop? claims linked to sources/concepts? reports/hypotheses clear enough for future frontend? missing fields creating refactor risk?
Default pytest and golden unchanged (live_network excluded); no public export/site/schema changes.
non_goals: Public atlas route or public-site UI.
rollback_plan: Remove atlas_coherence_report module and live_network test only.
category: Phase 3 / live research proof / Research Atlas product validation
